aus_extension.name: Nominal case alignment
aus_extension.gloss: Case-marking alignment of full (non-pronominal) noun phrases.

aus_extension.name: Pronominal case alignment
aus_extension.gloss: Case-marking alignment of independent personal pronouns.

aus_extension.name: Verbal person-marking alignment
aus_extension.gloss: Alignment of person marking (agreement/bound pronouns) on the verb.

aus_extension.name: Case-marking split between nouns and pronouns
aus_extension.gloss: Whether full nouns and pronouns use DIFFERENT case alignments (e.g. nouns ergative but pronouns nominative-accusative) — the classic Australian split.

aus_extension.name: Person/NP-type conditioned split ergativity
aus_extension.gloss: Whether ergativity is conditioned by the person/NP-type hierarchy (ergative on nouns/3rd, accusative on speech-act pronouns) — a hierarchy-based split.

aus_extension.name: Number of morphological cases
aus_extension.gloss: Size of the morphological case inventory on nouns.

aus_extension.name: Position of case affixes
aus_extension.gloss: Where case marking sits: suffix, prefix, clitic, tone, stem change.

aus_extension.name: Plurality in independent pronouns
aus_extension.gloss: How number is built into independent personal pronouns.

aus_extension.name: Inclusive/exclusive distinction
aus_extension.gloss: Whether 1st-person non-singular distinguishes inclusive ('we incl. you') from exclusive.

aus_extension.name: Inclusive/exclusive in verbal inflection
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the inclusive/exclusive distinction also appears in verbal person marking.

aus_extension.name: Sex-based vs non-sex-based gender
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the gender system is sex-based or non-sex-based (or absent).

aus_extension.name: Standard negation strategy
aus_extension.gloss: How declarative clauses are negated: affix, particle, negative auxiliary, etc.

aus_extension.name: Adposition type
aus_extension.gloss: Prepositions vs postpositions vs no dominant adpositions.

aus_extension.name: Predicative possession strategy
aus_extension.gloss: How 'X has Y' is expressed: locational, genitive, topic, conjunctional, or a 'have' verb.

aus_extension.name: Evidentiality coding
aus_extension.gloss: Whether grammatical evidentiality is marked, and its scope (direct/indirect).
